meaning: adj.轧轧响的,刺耳的,尖锐的
examples: This was their mother at her worst. Her voice was strident, she was ready to be angry at anyone.
He is a strident advocate of nuclear power.
The bluff strident words struck the note sailors understood, and they cheered him lustily.
Now, it has this strident connotation, you know, a little bit man-hating maybe or whatever.
She said the mother also expressed some rather strident views about the U.S. government.
